key_insight: Replacing the DFTB pairwise repulsive term with a many-body MACE correction yields a DFTB+MACE hybrid that improves magnesium-compound predictions while preserving access to electronic structure.
engineering_relevance: The hybrid model extends simulations of Mg-based materials to larger length and time scales than DFT, making it useful for adsorption, dynamics, phonons, and charge-transfer studies.
method: Train MACE on DFT minus DFTB energy/force residuals, excluding the pair-potential contribution.
Embed the learned many-body correction into a DFTB framework to replace the conventional repulsive potential.
Evaluate on MgO-CO2 adsorption, water-cluster MD, and fcc/bcc MgO phonons, comparing against DFTB, pure MACE, and DFT.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved accuracy over standard DFTB with only a moderate computational cost increase, with validation across structural relaxation, molecular dynamics, and phonon calculations.
watch_item: Performance may be limited where the original MACE training set does not cover the explored potential-energy surface, so broader training data may be needed.
paper_title: A Combined Tight Binding with Machine Learning Potential Model for Magnesium Compounds
